entries.definition:
gh–d–q great volume of water, heavy rain; fertile land,
comfortable living, generosity of character. Of this root, only غَدَق
ghadaq occurs once in the Qur’an.

غَدَق ghadaq [v. n. used adjectivally] abundant, plentiful,
copious (72:16) وَأَنْ لَوِ اسْتَقَامُوا عَلَى الطَّرِيقَةِ لَأَسْقَيْنَاهُمْ مَاءً غَدَقًا if they were
to be upright on the path [the Faith], We would give them to
drink water in abundance.
